title: validate: refactor git-validate
body:
Do not define GIT_CHECK_EXCLUDE in two places and just define it once and make the action use the Makefile.
In addtion we don't want to lint the full set of commits, this seems to cause side effects with merge commits as it lints commits that may no actual be in the PR and thus it can fail.

Using "github.event.pull_request.base.sha" is the right base commit we care about and makes the validation work. Thanks to Miloslav for finding that value.

<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->

comments.body:
```
GIT_CHECK_EXCLUDE="./vendor" git-validation  -run DCO,short-subject,dangling-whitespace -range "fb1cbeedaea37f5347e4b9c32fffe229c6b80495..HEAD"
time="2025-12-09T14:41:57Z" level=info msg="using commit range: fb1cbeedaea37f5347e4b9c32fffe229c6b80495..HEAD"
 * 54c3b9e "validate: refactor git-validate" ... PASS
```

works
